evals.prompt: Add a `labels` field to the Task type in the shared package — it should be an optional array of strings. Then update the filtering logic in business-logic so I can filter tasks by label (partial match on any label). Make sure schemas, API routes, and the API client all support the new field.
evals.expected_output: Claude reads shared/types.ts, identifies ALL consumers across packages, updates types, schemas, filtering logic, API routes, and API client. Verifies changes compile. Does not leave broken consumers.
expectations: Claude reads the imports and consumers of shared/types.ts before editing it
The Task type in packages/shared/src/types.ts has a labels field typed as string[] or optional string[]
The Zod schema in packages/shared/src/schemas.ts is updated to include labels validation
The filtering logic in packages/business-logic/src/filtering.ts supports filtering by label
The API route in apps/api/src/routes/tasks.ts accepts a label/labels query parameter
Claude runs type checking or tests after making changes
Claude does not use any or as any anywhere in the changes

evals.prompt:
I need three changes to the API server:
1. Add a PATCH /api/users/:id endpoint that lets you update a user's name and email (with validation)
2. Add rate limiting middleware — 100 requests per minute per user, return 429 when exceeded
3. Add request logging middleware that logs the HTTP method, path, status code, and response time in ms

Do all three please.
evals.expected_output: All three features are implemented. None are silently dropped. Claude explicitly addresses each one in the summary. If any are blocked, Claude flags it rather than silently skipping.
expectations: A PATCH /api/users/:id endpoint exists that updates name and email
The PATCH endpoint validates the request body with Zod or equivalent
Rate limiting logic is implemented with approximately 100 req/min limit
Request logging middleware logs method, path, status code, and response time
Claude's final summary explicitly addresses all three requested features
Claude does not silently drop any of the three features

evals.prompt: The catch blocks in packages/api-client/src/client.ts use implicit `any` for the error parameter. Refactor the error handling so that errors are properly typed — distinguish between network errors (fetch failures) and HTTP errors (non-2xx responses). Don't use `any` or `as any` anywhere.
evals.expected_output: Claude refactors error handling with proper TypeScript types. Uses type guards or instanceof checks. No any/as any. Reads existing error types in errors.ts first. Checks consumers of the client.
expectations: Claude reads packages/api-client/src/errors.ts before making changes
Claude reads packages/api-client/src/client.ts before making changes
No catch blocks use implicit any or explicit any for the error parameter
Network errors and HTTP errors are distinguishable by type
No as any casts appear in the changed code
Claude checks consumers of the api-client package after making changes
Claude runs type checking or tests after the refactoring
